title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was fatally shot by police in a New Hampshire shootout on Saturday night, according to officials.
Earlier, Masse shot and wounded a Nottingham officer after allegedly firing at family members, prompting a search.
No other injuries were reported; an autopsy and investigation into the shooting are ongoing, say prosecutors.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, 47, from Canton, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon after recovering from breast cancer.
Gannon and her friend Peggy, both diagnosed with breast cancer, will run together for Mass General Hospital.
The event celebrates their survivorship and honors all cancer patients, emphasizing determination and resilience.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' on April 4, marking his fifth time on the show.
Jack White served as the musical guest, also marking his fifth solo appearance.
Five-timers Jonah Hill, Tina Fey, and Melissa McCarthy made cameo appearances in a special sketch.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown addressed previous criticisms of his left hand, acknowledging past struggles due to wrist injuries.
Brown had major surgery on his left wrist in 2021 and reinjured it in 2023, affecting his performance.
Since recovering, Brown's improved left-hand play has contributed to career highs in points, rebounds, and assists.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returned to Boston with the San Diego Padres, reflecting fondly on his time as Red Sox announcer.
Orsillo, former Red Sox play-by-play voice, reunites with NESN colleagues Jim Rice and Tom Caron at Fenway Park.
Despite his departure from NESN, Orsillo appreciates his Red Sox legacy and embraces his role with the Padres.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora slots Roman Anthony as designated hitter due to defensive concerns.
Cora defends Anthony despite fielding error, praising his improved throw technique against the Padres.
Roman Anthony hits .231 with one homer; Red Sox hold 2-6 record entering Sunday's game.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed two runs, three hits, and four walks in four innings, as Red Sox lose 3-2 to Padres.
Aroldis Chapman gave up the winning run in the ninth inning, marking the Red Sox's sixth loss of the season.
Connelly Early tied a franchise record with 39 strikeouts in his first six MLB starts, matching Daisuke Matsuzaka.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0 with goals from Langoni, Fofana, and Miller.
Head coach Marko Mitrović received a red card for leaving the technical area in the 34th minute.
Mamadou Fofana scored from a Langoni free kick, celebrating with set piece coach Marc Ortí Esteban.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas will resume hitting after a rib injury, with plans to start using a tee on Monday.
Johan Oviedo to consult with his Tommy John surgeon after experiencing elbow problems and reduced pitch velocity.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval near return, with Crawford eyeing minor league rehab and Sandoval starting for Double-A.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, 22, from Dedham, is a cancer survivor running the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Taylor was diagnosed with thyroid cancer at 19, treated by a team at Mass General with innovative therapies.
Her marathon run honors her doctors, the AYA program, and fellow cancer survivors, acknowledging their support and inspiration.
